anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
Your static analyzer is failing: it projects 0 calls despite 26 active timers, creating a false "LOW" risk.

**(a) Runaway Loop:** Anthropic uses a `/tmp/` lockfile. **(b) Missing Caps:** Relying entirely on fragile client-side locks. No provider-side hard billing caps are mentioned.
**(c) 2x Blow-up:** If Gemini doubles its expected rate, it rapidly exhausts its 100-call limit, exponentially increasing daily spend.
**(d) Invisible Burn:** Gemini. The 100-call limit has "plenty of headroom," meaning recursive `audit_fire_debate` logic will silently
ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Blow-up scenarios:**

(a) **Runaway loops:** Anthropic cap relies on `/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt` — `/tmp` is volatile. Container restart, reboot, or tmpfs wipe resets the lockfile → multiple Opus fires/day at ~$2 each. Also no file-lock = race condition if two timers fire concurrently.

(b) **Missing caps:** Projected calls=0 despite 26 timers — auditor isn't actually parsing call rates, so unknown callers are invisible. No monthly/burst cap, only daily. No cap on Haiku/other Anthropic models if reintroduced.

(c) **2× rate:** Gemini at 200/day still under 100-cap? No — would breach silently if cap check has off-by-one or is bypassed. Opus at 2/day = ~$60/month from one stuck timer.

(d) **Most likely silent burn:** `audit_fire_debate` — uses Gemini cap pool, multi-turn debates ca
ai_consensus.grok:
**Blow-up risks:**

(a) **Runaway loops**: Anthropic lockfile (/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt) fails under concurrent pods or /tmp cleanup → unlimited Opus calls ($2 each). Gemini's hard-coded cap can be bypassed by new code paths.

(b) **Missing daily caps**: Only Anthropic/Gemini are capped. Any other provider or re-added Haiku has zero enforcement.

(c) **2x rate**: Anthropic jumps to 2 calls/day ($4/day, $1.5k/yr). Negligible for Gemini at 200 calls.

(d) **Stealth burn**: **Gemini** — 100/day cap is generous, usage hidden inside `audit_fire_debate`, no real-time visibility. 26 timers likely contain the next uncapped caller.

Overall: LOW today, HIGH once trading logic is added.
